goal: Define the document-only Auth/Persistence Readiness Contract after Approval Readiness without implementing auth, persistence, approval records, task creation, or Builder calls.
claims: BP-062 defines only a document-level Auth/Persistence Readiness Contract after Approval Readiness.
BP-062 keeps auth implementation, identity provider integration, persistence, approval records, task creation, execution, and live Builder calls blocked.
BP-062 does not implement UI, auth, secrets, DB, persistence, approval action, or Builder integration.
assumptions: Approval Readiness Candidate output is the required source for later Auth/Persistence Readiness input.
Auth and persistence can be assessed as boundaries before any implementation exists.
Effective approval needs later explicit identity, persistence, audit, and task-create readiness decisions.
dod: The document defines required input, output, gates, blocked behavior, and non-goals.
The document explicitly prevents auth implementation, persistence, approval recording, Task Create, Execute, Approve, Push, Deploy, secrets, DB, and live Builder calls.
stop_conditions: Runtime, fixture, package, or tool changes become necessary
Auth implementation, persistence, approval recording, live Builder, DB, deploy, execution, task creation, approve, or push becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-061 identified Auth/Persistence Readiness Contract as the next safe boundary after the local Approval Readiness chain.
